frames: The agent is initially low to the ground, with its body slightly elevated and all six legs spread out, making contact.
It shifts its weight, causing the body to dip slightly, and some legs appear to lift or drag briefly.
The agent's body lowers further, almost touching the ground, with its legs splayed wide.
It then begins to slowly raise its central body, preparing for a more upright stance.
The agent achieves a slightly higher, more stable posture, with all legs firmly planted on the checkered surface.
It initiates a slow forward movement by lifting its front-left leg, maintaining a low center of gravity.
The agent continues to crawl forward, now lifting its front-right leg in a slow, deliberate gait.
Its movement is smooth but very slow, resembling a cautious, low-to-the-ground crawl.
